prompt: Give me a short pep talk before I start a hard task I have been putting off all morning.
response:
Alright, listen up.

That thing you've been avoiding? It's not getting any bigger. *You're* just giving it more airtime in your head than it deserves. The version of this task in your mind is always worse than the actual doing of it.

Here's the deal: you don't have to feel ready. You don't have to want to. You just have to start — badly, messily, imperfectly. Fifteen minutes of ugly progress beats another hour of dreading it.

Open the thing. Take the first tiny step. Momentum is on the other side of that first move, and so is the version of you who gets to feel proud instead of guilty tonight.

Go. You've got this.
